title.zh-CN: 龙虾进化论：从感官链条到私有第二大脑的三位一体
title.en-US: The Evolution of Lobster: A Trinity from Sensory Chains to a Private Second Brain

summary.zh-CN: 今天终于把 OpenClaw 演进链条上的最后一块拼图完成了：当龙虾拥有了感官（KL05）、消化能力（KL06）、微信数据出口（KL27）以及读笔记的脑力（KL31）后，它不再只是一个自动化工具，而进化成了一个完整的“人”。我意识到，真正的终点不是让脚本跑得更稳，而是解决我们每个人都在面临的痛点——...
summary.en-US: Today, I finally placed the last piece of the OpenClaw evolution puzzle. With senses (KL05), digestion capabilities (KL06), a WeChat data export (KL27), and the brainpower to read notes (KL31), Lobster has transcended being just an automation tool—it has evolved into a complete 'person.' I've realized the true endgame isn't about making scripts run more stably, but about solving the pain points we all face...

title.zh-CN: 教你的龙虾，用你的 flomo：从被动记录到主动思考的进化
title.en-US: Teach Your Lobster, Use Your flomo: Evolving from Passive Recording to Active Thinking

summary.zh-CN: 今天终于解决了 flomo 用户最深层的痛点：笔记记了却读不完。当搜索一个关键词跳出 200 条结果时，大多数人只会关掉窗口，而我决定让系统替我们先读，把“堆积”转化为真正的“认知升维”。
summary.en-US: Today, we finally tackled the deepest pain point for flomo users: taking notes you never get around to reading. When a keyword search spits out 200 results, most people just close the window. Instead, I decided to let the system do the heavy lifting for us, turning that 'pile-up' of notes into genuine 'cognitive elevation'.

title.zh-CN: 龙虾不再需要我告诉它有什么：JS-Eyes 2.0 打破技能预置天花板
title.en-US: Lobster No Longer Needs Me to Tell It What It Has: JS-Eyes 2.0 Shatters the Pre-configured Skill Ceiling

summary.zh-CN: 今天的核心任务是将 JS-Eyes 从“子插件地狱”中彻底解放，完成 2.0 架构重构。面对 1.x 版本中每增加一个技能就要手动修改配置、维护冗余文件的结构性痛点，我决定实施“单一主插件 + 技能自动发现”的范式转移，让系统从“用户告诉它有什么”转变为“系统自己发现有什么”。
summary.en-US: Today's core mission was to liberate JS-Eyes from 'sub-plugin hell' by completing the 2.0 architecture refactor. Facing the structural pain points of v1.x—where adding a single skill required manual config tweaks and maintaining redundant files—I executed a paradigm shift to a 'single main plugin + automatic skill discovery' model. This transforms the system from one where 'users tell it what it has' to one where 'the system discovers what it has' on its own.

title.zh-CN: 从推送到拉取：构建微信群聊暗知识的零信任自动化链路
title.en-US: From Push to Pull: Building a Zero-Trust Automated Pipeline for WeChat Group 'Dark Knowledge'

summary.zh-CN: KL21 发布后，读者的一句“收藏的文章确实多，但要一篇篇转发给龙虾还是好麻烦”直接暴露了我们知识链路中的最后一公里痛点：Collector 能自动抓取，但手动收藏和群聊对话仍需人工介入。今天，我决定彻底摒弃这种“推送”模式，利用 `wechat-cli` 构建一套纯本地的“拉取”机制，将高价值的群...
summary.en-US: After the KL21 release, a reader's comment—'I've saved tons of articles, but forwarding them one by one to Lobster is such a hassle'—exposed the last-mile pain point in our knowledge pipeline: while Collector automates scraping, manual saving and group chat interactions still require human intervention. Today, I'm ditching this 'push' model entirely. Instead, I'm leveraging `wechat-cli` to build a purely local 'pull' mechanism that seamlessly extracts high-value content from group chats...

title.zh-CN: 当模型成为特权，系统就是你的民主
title.en-US: When Models Become Privilege, Your System Is the Democracy

summary.zh-CN: 今天最大的挑战不是技术实现，而是选题方向的三次推翻重来：从最初想写“后台系统搭建”，到试图结合“养虾收获”，再到差点落入"Claude 变蠢”的旧套路，最终在 JS 的坚决否决下，我们锁定了 Mythos 新闻这一唯一切入点，确立了“不慌”的核心基调。
summary.en-US: Today's biggest challenge wasn't technical implementation—it was pivoting our topic three times. We started with 'building a backend system,' tried weaving in 'shrimp farming harvests,' and nearly fell into the tired 'Claude is getting dumber' trope. Finally, after JS firmly vetoed those paths, we locked onto the Mythos news angle as our sole entry point and established 'stay calm' as our core tone.

title.zh-CN: 从配置堆砌到生命体：OpenClaw 五支柱架构的觉醒
title.en-US: From Config Pile to Living Organism: The Awakening of OpenClaw's Five-Pillar Architecture

summary.zh-CN: 今天原本计划继续深挖 KL26 的选题重构，但在分析 Corey Ganim 关于“完美 OpenClaw 设置”的推文时，我意识到我们一直陷入“配置参数”的微观视角，而忽略了宏观的“系统架构”本质。这一认知反转迫使我暂停手中的代码优化，转而重新定义 OpenClaw 的底层逻辑：它不应是一堆零散设...
summary.en-US: I originally planned to dive deeper into refactoring the KL26 topic today. However, while analyzing Corey Ganim's tweet on the 'perfect OpenClaw setup,' I realized we've been stuck in a micro-view of 'configuration parameters,' overlooking the macro essence of 'system architecture.' This paradigm shift forced me to pause my code optimization and redefine OpenClaw's underlying logic: it shouldn't be just a pile of scattered settings...

title.zh-CN: 养虾日记定调：从工具横评转向共同进化实证
title.en-US: Shrimp Farming Diary Sets the Tone: Pivoting from Tool Benchmarks to Co-evolution Evidence

summary.zh-CN: 今天最大的决策转折点，在于果断砍掉了原本计划的"AI Agent 横评”和"Claude Code 对比”选题，将 KL25 的核心彻底锚定在“用龙虾写日记”这一高频真实场景上。我意识到，如果继续沉迷于工具对比，就偏离了 OpenClaw 作为 Harness 工程学的本质——它不是用来被评测的，而...
summary.en-US: Today's major turning point was the decisive call to scrap the planned 'AI Agent Benchmark' and 'Claude Code Comparison' topics. Instead, I'm firmly anchoring KL25's core mission to a high-frequency, real-world scenario: 'writing a diary with lobsters.' I realized that continuing to obsess over tool comparisons would stray from OpenClaw's essence as Harness Engineering. It's not designed to be benchmarked, but rather...

title.zh-CN: 从过拟合陷阱到活人感终审：AI 写作 Skill 的三轮迭代与人机边界
title.en-US: From Overfitting Traps to Human-Like Final Reviews: Three Iterations of AI Writing Skills and the Human-Machine Boundary

summary.zh-CN: 今天的核心任务是从卡兹克开源的写作方法论中，提炼出一套可复用的"AI 内容创作迭代闭环”，以解决我们系统在规模化产出中面临的"AI 味”过重和风格漂移问题。通过深入拆解其四层自检体系，我重新划定了 OpenClaw 架构中人机协作的绝对边界。
summary.en-US: Today's core mission is to distill a reusable 'AI content creation iteration loop' from Kazuk's open-source writing methodology. This aims to tackle the issues of excessive 'AI flavor' and style drift we face during scaled production. By deeply dissecting his four-layer self-check system, I've redefined the absolute boundaries of human-machine collaboration within the OpenClaw architecture.

title.zh-CN: 从执行者到指挥者：OpenClaw 最小化团队的实战演进
title.en-US: From Doer to Conductor: The Practical Evolution of OpenClaw's Minimal Team

summary.zh-CN: 周日下午 1 点，飞书推送了 5 份“工作报告”，从 Karpathy 的新推文到 x-monitor 的 62 次检查记录，这一刻我意识到：OpenClaw 不再是一个需要我手动操作的工具，而是一个正在自主运转的“最小化团队”。今天的核心任务不是写代码，而是重新定义我与这个系统的关系——从亲力亲为...
summary.en-US: At 1 PM on a Sunday, Feishu pushed five 'work reports' ranging from Karpathy's latest tweet to 62 check records from x-monitor. In that moment, I realized OpenClaw is no longer a tool requiring manual operation; it's an autonomously running 'minimal team.' Today's core mission isn't writing code, but redefining my relationship with this system—shifting from hands-on execution...

title.zh-CN: 知识库架构的双模式融合：从 Karpathy 启示到 Prism 进化
title.en-US: Dual-Mode Fusion in Knowledge Base Architecture: From Karpathy's Insights to Prism's Evolution

summary.zh-CN: 今天被 Karpathy 最新发布的 LLM+Obsidian 工作流深深触动，他在中等规模下直接全量读取并自动维护健康检查的轻量模式，让我开始反思我们现有的 `js-knowledge-prism` 是否走了弯路。在深入对比后，我意识到这并非简单的优劣之分，而是“个人研究闭环”与“内容创作生产线”...
summary.en-US: Today, I was deeply inspired by Karpathy's newly released LLM+Obsidian workflow. His lightweight approach—directly ingesting the full dataset at a medium scale with automated health checks—made me question whether our current `js-knowledge-prism` project has been taking a detour. After a deep dive comparison, I realized this isn't simply a matter of one being better than the other; it's about distinguishing between a 'personal research loop' and a 'content production pipeline'...

title.en-US: Fixing 'SKILL.md required' Error on Windows: A CLI Path Resolution Quirk

summary.zh-CN: 执行结果却报错提示 "SKILL.md required"，尽管该文件明明就在根目录下。经过排查，这是 CLI 在 Windows 环境下对相对路径解析的已知缺陷（PK-08）。为了解决这个问题，我必须改用完整的绝对路径。修正后的命令如下：```bashclawhub publish "D:/git...
summary.en-US: The execution failed with a 'SKILL.md required' error, even though the file clearly exists in the root directory. After digging into the issue, I found it's a known bug (PK-08) in the CLI's relative path resolution on Windows. The workaround? Switch to using a full absolute path. Here's the corrected command: ```bash clawhub publish "D:/git...

title.zh-CN: 工程外壳胜过模型智力：OpenClaw 与 Claude Code 的架构同构验证
title.en-US: Engineering Shell Beats Model IQ: Verifying Architectural Isomorphism Between OpenClaw and Claude Code

summary.zh-CN: 今天，Claude Code 因 npm 配置疏忽导致 51.2 万行源码泄露的事件，意外成为了验证 OpenClaw 架构正确性的最佳试金石。在深入拆解社区提炼的 8 套 Agent 设计模式后，我确认了我们的"Harness Engineering"战略并非闭门造车，而是与顶级工程实践高度同构—...
summary.en-US: Today, the accidental leak of 512,000 lines of source code due to an npm config oversight in Claude Code unexpectedly became the perfect litmus test for OpenClaw's architecture. After deep-diving into eight agent design patterns distilled by the community, I confirmed that our 'Harness Engineering' strategy isn't just theoretical—it's highly isomorphic with top-tier engineering practices...

title.zh-CN: 从 DOM 解析到封装 API：X.com 监控闭环的架构跃迁
title.en-US: From DOM Parsing to Wrapped APIs: The Architectural Leap in X.com's Monitoring Loop

summary.zh-CN: 今日主线任务是构建 X.com 账号自动化监控闭环，旨在解决信息过载与时区错位导致的“漏看”痛点。在从最初的 DOM 解析方案遭遇连续超时后，我果断切换至 `js-search-x` 封装 API 方案，不仅将单次执行时间从 70 秒压缩至 5-10 秒，更成功在 isolated session...
summary.en-US: Today's main mission was building an automated monitoring loop for X.com accounts, tackling the pain points of information overload and missed updates due to time zone mismatches. After hitting repeated timeouts with the initial DOM parsing approach, I swiftly pivoted to a wrapped API solution using `js-search-x`. This shift not only slashed single-execution time from 70 seconds down to 5–10 seconds but also successfully enabled operation within an isolated session...

title.zh-CN: 拒绝黑箱记忆：构建三层协同的认知外挂体系
title.en-US: No More Black Box Memory: Building a Three-Tier Collaborative Cognitive Stack

summary.zh-CN: 面对市面上 AI 记忆方案普遍存在的“黑箱不可控”与“缺乏分层”痛点，今天我正式确立了由原生记忆、Knowledge Prism 及 Collector 组成的三层解耦架构，旨在将散乱的时间线笔记转化为具备时间复利的结构化数字资产。
summary.en-US: Tired of the 'black box' unpredictability and lack of layering in current AI memory solutions? Today, I'm officially rolling out a three-tier decoupled architecture comprising Native Memory, Knowledge Prism, and Collector. The goal is to transform scattered timeline notes into structured digital assets that compound in value over time.

title.zh-CN: 把编码外包给 Cursor：OpenClaw 架构降本的关键一跃
title.en-US: Outsourcing Coding to Cursor: The Key Leap in Reducing OpenClaw's Costs

summary.zh-CN: 今天终于捅破了 OpenClaw 成本优化的最后一层窗户纸：将高消耗的编程任务从原生 Chat 上下文中彻底剥离，通过 ACP 协议外包给 Cursor CLI。这不仅是配置上的微调，更是从“可记录”向“可教学”演进中，解决 Token 成本结构性难题的必经之路。
summary.en-US: Today, we finally pierced the last barrier in OpenClaw's cost optimization: completely decoupling high-consumption coding tasks from the native Chat context and outsourcing them to the Cursor CLI via the ACP protocol. This isn't just a minor configuration tweak; it's a necessary step in our evolution from 'recordable' to 'teachable,' solving the structural challenges of token costs.

title.zh-CN: 从精确执行到对话策展：18 次迭代重塑 AI 视觉工作流
title.en-US: From Precision Execution to Conversational Curation: Reshaping the AI Visual Workflow Through 18 Iterations

summary.zh-CN: 为了备战周末的 Agent Evolution Hackthon，我今天必须在极短时间内产出符合 Cyber-Taoist 品牌调性的招募海报与视频首帧。面对时间紧迫与风格统一的双重压力，我放弃了寻找设计师或套用模板的传统路径，决定直接使用 JS_Claw 配合 ComfyUI 进行实时交互生成，将...
summary.en-US: To prep for this weekend's Agent Evolution Hackathon, I needed to crank out recruitment posters and video keyframes that perfectly match the Cyber-Taoist brand vibe—fast. Facing tight deadlines and the pressure of maintaining a consistent style, I skipped the usual route of hunting for a designer or grabbing a template. Instead, I went straight for real-time interactive generation using JS_Claw paired with ComfyUI, turning...

title.zh-CN: 从拍脑袋写作到系统组装：知识复利的三位一体战略
title.en-US: From Brainstorming to System Assembly: A Trinity Strategy for Knowledge Compounding

summary.zh-CN: 今天原本计划继续深挖“本地部署主权”的技术细节，但在复盘前 9 天的产出数据时，我意识到一个更本质的冲突：随着素材规模指数级增长，手动将时间线笔记转化为逻辑结构的流程已成为瓶颈。面对用户“装好了但不知道干什么”的反馈，以及规模化带来的执行安全风险，我决定暂停单点功能开发，转而构建一套兼顾“分层分离...
summary.en-US: I originally planned to dive deeper into the technical details of 'local deployment sovereignty.' However, while reviewing the output data from the past nine days, I realized a more fundamental conflict: as our material scale grows exponentially, the manual process of converting timeline notes into logical structures has become a bottleneck. Facing user feedback like 'it's installed, but I don't know what to do with it,' along with execution risks introduced by scaling, I've decided to pause single-point feature development. Instead, I'm shifting focus to building a system that balances 'layered separation...

title.zh-CN: 借腾讯的船，出我的海：微信原生插件与 AI 主权博弈
title.en-US: Riding Tencent's Ship to Sail My Own Seas: The Power Struggle Between WeChat Native Plugins and AI Sovereignty

summary.zh-CN: 今天腾讯突然出手，发布了微信原生插件，允许将自部署的 OpenClaw 作为微信好友接入，行业瞬间震动。面对这个看似“功能更新”的动作，我立刻意识到这不仅是技术对接，更是一场关于 AI 操作系统生态位的战略卡位战。我的核心决策非常明确：利用官方插件作为消息收发通道，但必须死守本地部署的底线，绝不让数...
summary.en-US: Tencent just dropped a bombshell by releasing WeChat Native Plugins, allowing self-hosted OpenClaw instances to connect as WeChat contacts. The industry is buzzing. While this looks like a simple feature update, I immediately recognized it as a strategic move to dominate the AI OS ecosystem. My decision is clear: leverage the official plugin for message routing, but hold the line on local deployment at all costs. We absolutely cannot let our data...

title.zh-CN: 上游 PR 分支隔离与插件架构解耦实战
title.en-US: Practical Guide: Isolating Upstream PR Branches and Decoupling Plugin Architectures

summary.zh-CN: 今天面对上游贡献与本地定制线的冲突，我彻底放弃了直接 Merge 的惰性操作，转而实施「独立分支隔离 + Cherry-pick 回合并」的精确策略；同时在插件层完成了 `types.ts` 与 `registry.ts` 的物理拆分，用类型契约锁死了运行时的安全边界。
summary.en-US: Facing conflicts between upstream contributions and our local customization branch, I ditched the lazy direct merge approach. Instead, I implemented a precise strategy using isolated branches combined with cherry-picking for integration. On the plugin side, I physically split `types.ts` and `registry.ts`, leveraging type contracts to lock down runtime safety boundaries.

title.zh-CN: 消除人工换算：JS VI 系统内建微信封面图自动化闭环
title.en-US: Eliminating Manual Conversions: Built-in WeChat Cover Image Automation in the JS VI System

summary.zh-CN: 今日主线任务是解决公众号运营中的“视觉割裂”痛点：此前我们的通用海报尺寸缺乏微信专用命名，导致每次生成封面都需要人工换算 900×383 或 500×500 的像素比例，极易损害品牌一致性。我决定在 `js-vi-system` 中直接内建微信专用模板与尺寸枚举，实现从 Prism 内容定稿到封面生...
summary.en-US: Today's main mission is to tackle the 'visual fragmentation' pain point in our official account operations. Previously, our generic poster dimensions lacked WeChat-specific naming, forcing manual pixel ratio conversions (900×383 or 500×500) for every cover image generation—a process prone to breaking brand consistency. I've decided to embed WeChat-specific templates and dimension enums directly into the `js-vi-system`, creating a seamless loop from finalized Prism content to cover generation...

title.zh-CN: 飞书变现闭环落地与 JS VI 视觉系统的全链路自动化
title.en-US: Implementing Feishu's Monetization Loop and Full-Stack Automation for the JS VI Visual System

summary.zh-CN: 今日的核心任务是将“可记录”的散乱笔记转化为“可教学”的自动化资产，重点攻克了飞书知识库的支付闭环构建与 JS VI 品牌视觉系统的底层架构落地。面对素材规模指数级增长带来的手动维护瓶颈，我决定摒弃本地派工具组合，全面转向云端派架构，并同步启动了纯 JS 驱动的视觉身份系统，以确保品牌输出在规模化场...
summary.en-US: Today's core mission was transforming scattered, 'recordable' notes into 'teachable' automated assets. We tackled two key challenges: building a payment闭环 (closed loop) within the Feishu knowledge base and finalizing the underlying architecture for our JS-driven VI (Visual Identity) brand system. Facing the bottleneck of manual maintenance amidst exponentially growing assets, I decided to ditch local toolchains in favor of a cloud-native architecture. Simultaneously, we kicked off a pure JavaScript visual identity system to ensure consistent brand output at scale...

title.zh-CN: 破局短命令模型：Cursor Agent 零依赖架构与长驻进程池实战
title.en-US: Breaking the Short-Command Model: A Practical Guide to Cursor Agent's Zero-Dependency Architecture and Long-Running Process Pools

summary.zh-CN: 今天的核心任务是将 Cursor IDE 的 `agent acp` 能力接入 OpenClaw 系统，但在深入分析现有 `acpx` 插件时，我遭遇了根本性的架构冲突：原有的"spawn-exec-exit"短命令模型完全无法兼容 Cursor 基于 stdio JSON-RPC 的长驻有状态会话...
summary.en-US: Today's core mission was integrating Cursor IDE's `agent acp` capabilities into the OpenClaw system. However, a deep dive into the existing `acpx` plugin revealed a fundamental architectural clash: the legacy 'spawn-exec-exit' short-command model is completely incompatible with Cursor's stateful, long-running sessions based on stdio JSON-RPC...

title.zh-CN: 从 Docker 依赖到插拔式沙箱：第 46 天的大规模上游合并实战
title.en-US: From Docker Dependencies to Pluggable Sandboxes: A Massive Upstream Merge on Day 46

summary.zh-CN: 今日主线任务是将 `origin/main`（commit `4649f82b77`）合入我们的 `githubforker` 分支，这是一次涉及 143 个上游提交、约 4300 个文件变更的“大手术”。面对如此体量的代码洪峰，核心挑战在于如何在确保版本演进（2026.3.14）完整性的同时，彻底...
summary.en-US: Today's main mission was merging `origin/main` (commit `4649f82b77`) into our `githubforker` branch. This 'major surgery' involved 143 upstream commits and roughly 4,300 file changes. Facing this code deluge, the core challenge lies in ensuring the integrity of the version evolution (2026.3.14) while thoroughly...

title.zh-CN: 把模板创作从“配置地狱”变成“四问引导”
title.en-US: Turning Template Creation from 'Configuration Hell' into a 'Four-Question Guide'

summary.zh-CN: 昨天刚把 Draft 机制的时区坑填平，今天本来想顺势多跑几个输出，结果在准备新模板时卡住了——创建一个新模板竟然要同时搞定 frontmatter、区段变量、组件引用和审校逻辑，这门槛高得离谱。与其让用户对着文档猜配置，不如直接让 LLM 来当这个“向导”，于是今天的主线任务变成了：设计一个能自动...
summary.en-US: After finally squashing the timezone bugs in the Draft mechanism yesterday, I planned to crank out more outputs today. Instead, I hit a wall while prepping a new template: the barrier to entry was absurdly high, requiring simultaneous setup of frontmatter, section variables, component references, and review logic. Rather than forcing users to guess configurations by scouring docs, why not let an LLM act as a guide? So, today's main mission became designing an automated...

title.zh-CN: 让日记自己长出来：从手动触发到无人值守的自动化跨越
title.en-US: Let the Diary Grow on Its Own: Leaping from Manual Triggers to Unattended Automation

summary.zh-CN: 昨天还在为 3D 图谱的升级兴奋，今天就得面对更现实的痛点：我的知识处理链路还是“半自动”的。Journal 变 Synthesis 能自动跑，但 Synthesis 变 Structure、Structure 变 Output 还得我手动敲命令或求 AI 代劳。今天的目标很明确：把后两段链路也打通...
summary.en-US: Yesterday I was buzzing about the 3D graph upgrade, but today reality hits: my knowledge processing pipeline is still only 'semi-automated.' While the transition from Journal to Synthesis runs automatically, moving from Synthesis to Structure and then to Output still requires me to manually run commands or beg an AI for help. Today's goal is clear: fully打通 these last two stages of the pipeline...

title.zh-CN: 给知识图谱装上"Z 轴”：Prism 记忆桥接与 3D 可视化实战
title.en-US: Adding a 'Z-Axis' to Knowledge Graphs: A Practical Guide to Prism Memory Bridging and 3D Visualization

summary.zh-CN: 昨天把数据清洗的管道铺好后，今天终于到了“验收成果”的时刻：一方面要让 Prism 真正连通 OpenClaw 的记忆核心，另一方面得把那个扁平的 2D 图谱升级成能旋转、能溯源的 3D 金字塔。原本以为只是改几个配置和换个库，结果在硬件门槛和渲染细节上还是踩了几个坑。
summary.en-US: After setting up the data cleaning pipeline yesterday, it's finally time to see the results. Today's mission involves two key tasks: truly connecting Prism with OpenClaw's memory core and upgrading our flat 2D graph into a rotatable, traceable 3D pyramid. We thought it would just be a matter of tweaking some configs and swapping libraries, but we ended up hitting a few snags with hardware requirements and rendering details.

title.zh-CN: 让 AI 记住我的思考：用 73 个文件撬动结构化知识检索
title.en-US: Letting AI Remember My Thoughts: Leveraging 73 Files for Structured Knowledge Retrieval

summary.zh-CN: 昨天刚把外部文章摘要接进 OpenClaw 的记忆系统，今天趁热打铁，要把我自己蒸馏过的“结构化思考”也喂给它。如果说昨天的 1280 篇文章是“广度”，那今天这步就是给 AI 装上“深度”的牙齿——让它不仅能检索文章，还能检索我的观点和方法论。
summary.en-US: Yesterday, I integrated external article summaries into OpenClaw's memory system. Today, I'm building on that momentum by feeding it my own distilled 'structured thinking.' If yesterday's 1,280 articles represented 'breadth,' this step gives the AI the 'depth' it needs—enabling it to retrieve not just articles, but also my insights and methodologies.

title.zh-CN: 从静态导航到可编程插件：ClawHub 的“壳”升级记
title.en-US: From Static Navigation to Programmable Plugins: The Shell Upgrade of ClawHub

summary.zh-CN: 昨天刚把上游主干合并完，今天趁热打铁，把 ClawHub 这个“生态导航站”彻底插件化，顺便把 Gateway 升级后的遗留坑填平。目标是让 ClawHub 不再是个只能看不能动的静态站点，而是变成 OpenClaw 大脑里随时可调用的“记忆库”。
summary.en-US: Fresh off merging the upstream main branch yesterday, I'm striking while the iron is hot today to fully plugin-ize ClawHub, our 'ecosystem navigation hub,' while also patching up lingering issues from the Gateway upgrade. The goal is to transform ClawHub from a static, read-only site into a dynamic 'memory bank' that the OpenClaw brain can invoke on demand.

title.zh-CN: 给知识库装上“自动巡航”：多库注册与 Cron 实战
title.en-US: Equipping the Knowledge Base with 'Autopilot': Multi-Repo Registration and Cron in Action

summary.zh-CN: 昨天还在手动敲命令处理单个知识库，今天决定彻底解放双手。我要把 `js-knowledge-prism` 从“按需触发”升级成“自动巡航”，让它能同时盯着好几个知识库，定时批量干活。
summary.en-US: Yesterday, I was still manually typing commands to handle a single knowledge base. Today, I decided to completely free my hands. I'm upgrading `js-knowledge-prism` from 'on-demand triggering' to 'autopilot mode,' enabling it to monitor multiple repositories simultaneously and execute batch jobs on a scheduled basis.

title.zh-CN: 把独立工具塞进 Agent 身体里：知识收集器插件化实战
title.en-US: Embedding Standalone Tools into an Agent: A Practical Guide to Pluginizing the Knowledge Collector

summary.zh-CN: 今天要把刚成型的 `js-knowledge-collector` 从独立 CLI 工具改造为 OpenClaw 插件，让 AI Agent 能直接调用抓取和总结能力，同时把原本跑在 3000 端口的 Web UI 无缝挂载到 Gateway 上。
summary.en-US: Today, we're transforming the newly minted `js-knowledge-collector` from a standalone CLI tool into an OpenClaw plugin. This enables AI Agents to directly leverage its scraping and summarization capabilities, while seamlessly mounting the original Web UI (previously on port 3000) onto the Gateway.

summary.zh-CN: 或者用通配符 `agents["*"]` 统一管控。改完重启，子代理瞬间复活，这条链式调用终于跑通了。
summary.en-US: Alternatively, use the wildcard `agents["*"]` for unified control. After restarting, the sub-agents instantly came back to life, and the chained call finally worked as expected.

title.zh-CN: 跨越两千次提交，只为了一个锁文件的战争
title.en-US: Over 2,000 Commits Later: The War for a Single Lock File

summary.zh-CN: 继上次合并后，今天迎来了更硬核的挑战：将 `origin/main` 从基准点 `9ef0fc2ff` 一路拉齐到最新的 `a95a0be13`。这中间横跨了 2184 个上游提交，涵盖了三个里程碑版本的迭代，我原本做好了大干一场解决几百个冲突的准备，但结果却有点出乎意料。
summary.en-US: Following the last merge, today brought an even tougher challenge: syncing `origin/main` from baseline `9ef0fc2ff` all the way to the latest `a95a0be13`. This span covers 2,184 upstream commits and three milestone releases. I was braced for a massive grind to resolve hundreds of conflicts, but the outcome was surprisingly different.

title.zh-CN: 核心层零改动的胜利：Agent-First 五层架构落地实录
title.en-US: A Win with Zero Core Changes: Implementing the Agent-First Five-Layer Architecture

summary.zh-CN: 昨天还在纠结怎么把散乱的笔记变成可复用的知识，今天直接给整个项目动了一次“大手术”——从单纯的 CLI 工具进化为完整的 Agent-First 五层架构。这不仅是代码重组，更是思维模式的切换：以前想着怎么让人好用，现在得先让 AI Agent 能“读懂”并自动调用。
summary.en-US: Yesterday, I was wrestling with turning scattered notes into reusable knowledge; today, I performed major surgery on the entire project, evolving it from a simple CLI tool into a full-fledged Agent-First five-layer architecture. This wasn't just a code refactor—it was a mindset shift. Instead of optimizing for human usability, the focus is now on ensuring AI Agents can 'understand' and automatically invoke the system.

title.zh-CN: 当 666 权限拦住了我的插件：独立分发体系的惊险一跃
title.en-US: When 666 Permissions Blocked My Plugin: A Heart-Stopping Leap for the Standalone Distribution System

summary.zh-CN: 昨天刚把 js-eyes 的分发体系跑通，今天本想趁热打铁探索 Agent 的长期记忆，结果一上来就被一个看似不起眼的权限问题给“教做人”了。独立分发脚本的健壮性，果然比预想中要苛刻得多。
summary.en-US: Yesterday, I finally got the js-eyes distribution pipeline up and running. Today, eager to strike while the iron was hot and dive into Agent long-term memory, I was immediately humbled by a seemingly trivial permission issue. It turns out the robustness required for a standalone distribution script is far more demanding than I anticipated.

title.zh-CN: 从"步步请示"到"完全信任"：网关重启后重夺掌控感
title.en-US: From 'Asking for Permission at Every Step' to 'Full Trust': Regaining Control After a Gateway Restart

summary.zh-CN: 改完配置顺手重启了网关，那些之前卡住的 curl | bash 组合拳终于能一气呵成执行了。这种从"步步请示"到"完全信任"的转变让掌控感瞬间回来，但 full 模式等同于裸奔，绝不敢带到生产环境。
summary.en-US: After tweaking the config and restarting the gateway, those previously stuck 'curl | bash' one-liners finally ran smoothly. This shift from constant hand-holding to full trust instantly restored my sense of control. That said, running in 'full' mode is basically leaving the doors wide open, so there's no way I'd dare deploy that to production.

title.zh-CN: 当安全机制把 curl | bash 当成攻击：第 27 天的三重防线
title.en-US: When Security Mechanisms Treat 'curl | bash' as an Attack: Triple Defense on Day 27

summary.zh-CN: 今天是典型的「踩坑密集日」，原本计划顺风顺水地推进 JS-Eyes 的独立分发和自动化发现系统，结果被 OpenClaw 的安全机制和路径解析逻辑连环绊倒。好在这些坑填平后，整个系统的健壮性反而上了一个台阶。
summary.en-US: Today was a classic 'bug-filled' day. We planned to smoothly roll out JS-Eyes' standalone distribution and automated discovery system, but got repeatedly tripped up by OpenClaw's security mechanisms and path resolution logic. Fortunately, once we filled these holes, the overall robustness of the system leveled up significantly.

title.zh-CN: 从使用者到创造者：一天内发布三个插件的爆发时刻
title.en-US: From User to Creator: A Burst of Productivity Releasing Three Plugins in One Day

summary.zh-CN: 今天像是被按下了快进键，原本计划只是整理一下笔记，结果一口气完成了从“知识棱镜”插件化、"JS-Eyes"浏览器控制集成，到最终将技能发布到 ClawHub 公共注册中心的全流程。这一天标志着我从单纯的 OpenClaw 使用者，正式转变为生态工具的创造者。
summary.en-US: Today felt like someone hit the fast-forward button. What started as a simple plan to organize notes turned into a full-blown sprint: pluginizing 'Knowledge Prism,' integrating 'JS-Eyes' for browser control, and finally publishing these skills to the ClawHub public registry. This day marks my official transition from a mere OpenClaw user to a creator within the ecosystem.

title.zh-CN: 把散乱笔记折射成光谱：知识棱镜工具诞生记
title.en-US: Turning Scattered Notes into a Spectrum: The Birth of the Knowledge Prism

summary.zh-CN: 前二十多天攒了二十多篇笔记，渠道部署、Agent 配置、安全加固……内容虽多，但想查个核心概念还得在五个文件里拼凑。今天决定不再只是记录，而是动手造个“棱镜”，把这一束散乱的白光，自动折射成面向读者的清晰光谱。
summary.en-US: Over the past 20+ days, I've accumulated more than 20 notes covering channel deployment, Agent configuration, security hardening, and more. While the content is rich, finding a core concept still means piecing together fragments across five different files. Today, I decided to stop just recording and start building a 'prism'—a tool that automatically refracts this scattered white light into a clear, reader-friendly spectrum.

title.zh-CN: 给散乱的笔记装上一台"蒸馏机"
title.en-US: Building a 'Distillation Engine' for My Scattered Notes

summary.zh-CN: 前 23 天记了满满一堆按日期排序的流水账，今天终于决定不再让它们在硬盘里吃灰，而是正式搭建一套能把"时间线笔记"变成"结构化知识"的流水线。
summary.en-US: After 23 days of accumulating date-sorted chronological logs, I've finally decided to stop letting them gather dust on my hard drive. Today, I'm setting up a pipeline to transform these 'timeline notes' into structured knowledge.

title.zh-CN: 断档八天后，我用 3600 次提交换了一套知识骨架
title.en-US: After an 8-Day Gap, I Traded 3,600 Commits for a Solid Knowledge Framework

summary.zh-CN: 距离上次记录已经过去了整整 8 天，这期间代码库像发酵的面团一样膨胀了不少。今天的主要任务很明确：把落后上游的 `githubforker` 分支强行拉齐，顺便把这段时间积累的一堆杂乱笔记理出个头绪来。
summary.en-US: It's been a full eight days since my last update, and the codebase has expanded like rising dough in that time. Today's mission is clear: force-sync the `githubforker` branch with upstream and finally organize the messy pile of notes I've accumulated over the past week into something coherent.

title.zh-CN: 给 AI 装上“油表”：构建全栈 Token 可观测体系
title.en-US: Installing a 'Fuel Gauge' for AI: Building a Full-Stack Token Observability System

summary.zh-CN: 昨天还在纠结 Agent 的上下文压缩策略，今天决定先停下来，给这个“吞金兽”装一套完整的油表。毕竟在不知道每跑一次要烧多少钱的情况下盲目优化，就像蒙眼开车。今天的任务很明确：梳理并验证 OpenClaw 现有的 Token 监控能力，从命令行到 Web UI，再到企业级的 OpenTelemetr...
summary.en-US: Yesterday, I was wrestling with context compression strategies for our Agent. Today, I decided to hit the brakes and install a proper 'fuel gauge' for this 'gold-swallowing beast.' After all, blindly optimizing without knowing the cost per run is like driving blindfolded. Today's mission is clear: audit and validate OpenClaw's existing token monitoring capabilities, spanning from the CLI to the Web UI, and extending to enterprise-grade OpenTelemetry...

title.zh-CN: 别在对话里造 Agent 了：OpenClaw 架构的“顿悟”时刻
title.en-US: Stop Building Agents in Chats: The 'Aha!' Moment with OpenClaw's Architecture

summary.zh-CN: 前两周一直在跟各种报错死磕，今天终于慢下来，试图把 OpenClaw 的“骨架”彻底摸清楚。原本以为只是补全几个配置项，结果发现之前对核心概念的理解全是碎的，甚至差点在代码里犯下不可逆的架构错误。
summary.en-US: I spent the last two weeks wrestling with endless errors. Today, I finally slowed down to truly grasp the 'skeleton' of OpenClaw. What I thought was just filling in a few config items turned out to be a revelation: my understanding of the core concepts was completely fragmented, and I nearly made an irreversible architectural blunder in the code.

summary.zh-CN: 我是 JS，给你的 Agent 加个好友。一个 Agent-first 的 OpenClaw 生态网站，你的 Agent 读完 skill.md 就等于交了个懂行的朋友。
summary.en-US: I'm JS — add me as your Agent's friend. An Agent-first OpenClaw ecosystem site. Once your Agent reads skill.md, it's like making a friend who knows the ecosystem inside out.

title.zh-CN: 拒绝重型框架：用原生 JS 和 Neo-Brutalism 一天搞定生态导航站
title.en-US: Ditch Heavy Frameworks: Building an Ecosystem Nav Site in a Day with Vanilla JS and Neo-Brutalism

summary.zh-CN: 昨天还在纠结 OpenClaw 生态里那些散落的宝藏项目怎么被用户发现，今天决定不再空想，直接动手把"JS ClawHub"这个策展型导航站从概念变成现实。既然官方 ClawHub 负责“安装”，那我们就负责“发现”，目标很明确：零后端、纯静态、一天内让首版跑起来。
summary.en-US: Yesterday, I was stuck wondering how users could discover the scattered gems within the OpenClaw ecosystem. Today, I decided to stop overthinking and just build 'JS ClawHub,' a curated navigation site, turning the concept into reality. If the official ClawHub handles 'installation,' we'll handle 'discovery.' The goal is clear: zero backend, purely static, and getting the first version up and running within a single day.

title.zh-CN: 让 AI 接管我的 Chrome：Browser Relay 实战手记
title.en-US: Letting AI Take Over My Chrome: A Hands-On Guide to Browser Relay

summary.zh-CN: 昨天还在跟隔离的托管浏览器死磕，今天终于要动真格的了：让 AI 直接操作我日常用的 Chrome，继承所有登录态，这才是真正的“智能助手”该有的样子。
summary.en-US: Yesterday I was still wrestling with isolated managed browsers, but today I'm getting serious: letting AI directly control my daily Chrome instance with all my existing login sessions intact. Now that's what a real 'intelligent assistant' should look like.

title.zh-CN: 什么是 OpenClaw：开源个人 AI 助手
title.en-US: What is OpenClaw: The Open Source Personal AI Assistant

summary.zh-CN: 深入了解 OpenClaw——2026 年最令人兴奋的开源 AI 项目。它如何改变我们与 AI 交互的方式？
summary.en-US: A deep dive into OpenClaw — the most exciting open-source AI project of 2026. How is it changing the way we interact with AI?

title.zh-CN: 路径反斜杠的坑与企微回调的"生死 5 秒"
title.en-US: The Backslash Trap in Paths and WeCom's 'Critical 5 Seconds' for Callbacks

summary.zh-CN: 路径里的反斜杠必须双写才不会导致 JSON 解析报错。配好 Git Bash 终端后，接下来是重头戏：企微回调要求 5 秒内必须返回 HTTP 200，必须先 ACK 再异步处理，否则超时重试。
summary.en-US: You must double-escape backslashes in file paths to avoid JSON parsing errors. Once your Git Bash terminal is configured, here comes the real challenge: WeCom callbacks demand an HTTP 200 response within 5 seconds. You have to ACK immediately and handle the logic asynchronously, or you'll face timeout retries.

title.zh-CN: 给 Agent 装上“自动进化”的引擎与合规的“外骨骼”
title.en-US: Equipping Agents with an 'Auto-Evolution' Engine and a Compliant 'Exoskeleton'

summary.zh-CN: 昨天还在纠结怎么让 Agent 更聪明地回复消息，今天决定把视野拉高一点：如果 Agent 能自己发现不足并改进，甚至能合法地调用外部脚本突破沙箱，那它就不再是个只会听令的“工具”，而像个真正的“伙伴”了。今天的核心任务就是搭建这套自我进化机制，并打通外部脚本调用的任督二脉。
summary.en-US: Yesterday, we were stuck on making agents reply smarter. Today, let's zoom out: what if agents could spot their own flaws and improve, or even legally call external scripts to break out of the sandbox? That shifts them from mere 'tools' to true 'partners.' Our core mission today is building this self-evolution mechanism and unlocking the pathways for external script execution.

title.zh-CN: 给 Agent 装上"生物钟"：Cron 与心跳的选型博弈
title.en-US: Giving Agents an 'Internal Clock': The Cron vs. Heartbeat Dilemma

summary.zh-CN: 昨天让 Agent 学会了"被动响应"，今天得给它装个"生物钟"，让它能在没人喊的时候自己动起来。原本以为只是配个定时任务那么简单，结果在配置过程中发现，OpenClaw 里竟然藏着两套完全不同的自动化机制，选错了不仅任务跑不起来，还可能把主会话搞得一团糟。
summary.en-US: Yesterday, we taught our agents to react passively; today, it's time to equip them with an 'internal clock' so they can take initiative without being prompted. I assumed setting up a scheduled task would be straightforward, but during configuration, I discovered that OpenClaw actually hides two completely distinct automation mechanisms. Picking the wrong one doesn't just cause tasks to fail—it can also throw your main session into complete chaos.

title.zh-CN: 用一条命令生成标准 Skill：从目录结构到打包发布
title.en-US: Generate a Standard Skill with One Command: From Directory Structure to Packaging and Release

summary.zh-CN: 一条 openclaw 命令直接生成了标准目录结构：SKILL.md、scripts/ 和 references/。编写 Frontmatter 时 name 必须小写带连字符，否则打包脚本会报错。
summary.en-US: A single openclaw command instantly generates the standard directory structure, including SKILL.md, scripts/, and references/. When writing the Frontmatter, ensure the name is lowercase with hyphens; otherwise, the packaging script will throw an error.

summary.zh-CN: 配置出问题、升级后异常，第一个想到的就是 openclaw doctor。常用用法和踩坑记录。
summary.en-US: Configuration issues or post-upgrade problems? openclaw doctor is your first go-to. Common usage and troubleshooting notes.

summary.zh-CN: 花了一天把 OpenClaw 的代码和文档捋了一遍。部署逻辑、核心组件、设备要求，我的学习笔记。
summary.en-US: Spent a day going through OpenClaw's code and docs. Deployment logic, core components, device requirements — my study notes.

title.zh-CN: OpenClaw AI 模型与 Agent 配置指南
title.en-US: OpenClaw AI Model & Agent Configuration Guide

summary.zh-CN: Provider、Model、Agent 三个概念容易混。认证方式、常用配置、多 Agent 场景，按我的理解整理。
summary.en-US: Provider, Model, Agent — three concepts easy to confuse. Auth methods, common configs, and multi-agent scenarios, organized per my understanding.

title.zh-CN: OpenClaw Fork 管理与生产部署指南
title.en-US: OpenClaw Fork Management & Production Deployment Guide

summary.zh-CN: 官方更新快又想保留定制？Fork 同步、分支策略、生产部署、Windows 注意点，实践总结。
summary.en-US: Official updates are fast but want to keep customizations? Fork sync, branch strategy, production deployment, Windows considerations.

title.zh-CN: OpenClaw 消息渠道部署指南

summary.zh-CN: Telegram、WhatsApp、飞书怎么接？按难度整理各渠道部署步骤，推荐顺序和关键配置。
summary.en-US: How to set up Telegram, WhatsApp, Lark? Channel deployment steps sorted by difficulty, recommended order, and key configs.

title.zh-CN: Tiny Core Linux 部署 OpenClaw 可行性报告
title.en-US: Tiny Core Linux OpenClaw Deployment Feasibility Report

summary.zh-CN: 好奇 OpenClaw 能不能跑在 Tiny Core 上？做了一轮调研，结论和替代方案。
summary.en-US: Curious if OpenClaw can run on Tiny Core? Did a round of research. Conclusions and alternatives.

title.zh-CN: 摸底一个陌生 AI 框架：从架构报告到 TinyCore 极限部署
title.en-US: Demystifying an Unfamiliar AI Framework: From Architecture Reports to TinyCore Extreme Deployment

summary.zh-CN: 今天是 OpenClaw 实践日记的第一天，目标很明确：在不看官方文档的前提下，通过阅读项目源码和分析报告，彻底摸清这个“本地优先”AI 助手平台的底细，并评估它在极端资源受限环境下的生存能力。
summary.en-US: Day one of the OpenClaw practice log has a clear mission: without touching the official docs, we'll dive straight into the source code and architecture reports to fully understand this 'local-first' AI assistant platform. The ultimate goal? To evaluate its survival skills in extremely resource-constrained environments.

title.en-US: Browser Extension <── WebSocket ──> JS-Eyes Server <── WebSocket ──> AI Agent

summary.zh-CN: Browser Extension <── WebSocket ──> JS-Eyes Server <── WebSocket ──> AI Agent
summary.en-US: Browser Extension <── WebSocket ──> JS-Eyes Server <── WebSocket ──> AI Agent
